title: Cannot run container with podman-run when using an absolute path with the oci:path:reference transport
body:
### Issue Description

Cannot specify absolute paths using the `oci:path:reference` when running podman-run as root. 

### Steps to reproduce the issue

```
$ podman run -ti oci:$(realpath oci):ebpfsec /bin/bash
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 9c704ecd0c69 skipped: already exists
Copying blob ebf3da259b96 skipped: already exists
Copying config 6a6637cd5c done   |
Writing manifest to image destination
root@73813acadf58:/#
exit

$ sudo podman run -ti oci:$(realpath oci):ebpfsec /bin/bash
Error: parsing "localhost/home/amiculas/.local/share/stacker/oci": parsing named reference "localhost/home/amiculas/.local/share/stacker/oci": invalid reference format

$ podman run -ti oci:oci:ebpfsec /bin/bash
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 9c704ecd0c69 skipped: already exists
Copying blob ebf3da259b96 skipped: already exists
Copying config 6a6637cd5c done   |
Writing manifest to image destination
root@d11da658f631:/#

$ sudo podman run -ti oci:oci:ebpfsec /bin/bash
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 9c704ecd0c69 skipped: already exists
Copying blob ebf3da259b96 skipped: already exists
Copying config 6a6637cd5c done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
root@e4e90d8d9115:/#
```

### Describe the results you received

When specifying a relative path for the oci image, I can start the container as both my regular user and as root.
When specifying an absolute path for the oci image, the container only start when I run the command as a regular user.

### Describe the results you expected

I expected to be able to use the absolute path in the `oci:path:reference` syntax when running `podman-run` as root.

No

### Additional environment details

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body: What is the full output of `realpath oci`?

Yes. `podman run` implies copying the image to the local storage, and names in the local storage have a specific format (here in particular, `/.` is not allowed).

[The heuristic](https://github.com/containers/common/blob/247956145f8af73a9d28f4afd5e844c9eea5672d/libimage/pull.go#L272-L273) uses the user-specified path as the proposed local storage name, but that doesn’t always work.

Admittedly that’s not a great heuristic, but it’s also one that users might well rely on, so I don’t know that we can change it.

Short-term, I’d recommend creating a symlink with the desired c/storage name.

Longer-term, I think `podman pull` should get an option to specify the local name (`podman pull --to=my-working-image quay.io/base/image:9.8`); it seems to me that would be generally a nice abstraction. Whether to extend that to `podman run` (`podman run --pull-to=…`?) I’m less sure.

Alternatively, today already it should be possible to `skopeo copy oci:… containers-storage:[…]desired-name`, but the `[…]` part is non-obvious and ~must be determined from Podman’s debug logs, which is inconvenient, especially over longer terms as Podman and the `[…]` part might change.

@mtrmac thanks for the details. Do you know why this works (running it as a regular user):
```
$ podman run -ti oci:$(realpath oci):ebpfsec /bin/bash
````
and this fails (running it as root)?
```
$ sudo podman run -ti oci:$(realpath oci):ebpfsec /bin/bash
```
Turn the `podman` commands into `echo podman` commands to see the expanded output of `realpath`.

```
$ echo podman run -ti oci:$(realpath oci):ebpfsec /bin/bash
podman run -ti oci:/home/amiculas/.local/share/stacker/oci:ebpfsec /bin/bash
```
```
$ sudo echo podman run -ti oci:$(realpath oci):ebpfsec /bin/bash
podman run -ti oci:/home/amiculas/.local/share/stacker/oci:ebpfsec /bin/bash
```
I get the same output

Here are the commands without realpath:
```
$ podman run -ti oci:/home/amiculas/.local/share/stacker/oci:ebpfsec /bin/bash
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 9c704ecd0c69 skipped: already exists
Copying blob ebf3da259b96 skipped: already exists
Copying config 6a6637cd5c done   |
Writing manifest to image destination
root@0bdb8e2e6ef9:/#
exit
```
```
$ sudo podman run -ti oci:/home/amiculas/.local/share/stacker/oci:ebpfsec /bin/bash
Error: parsing "localhost/home/amiculas/.local/share/stacker/oci": parsing named reference "localhost/home/amiculas/.local/share/stacker/oci": invalid reference format
```

I don’t know, that behavior does not make much sense to me.

Are you running _different versions of `podman`_ in those environments? https://github.com/containers/common/commit/08fc0b450a7651dabcd7d913ed41a21eaf2272f4 might explain it.

yes, that was it:
```
$ podman --version
podman version 5.1.1
```
```
$ sudo podman --version
podman version 3.4.4
```
Thanks for your support!
